verses.text: 'O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you?' received ye the Spirit by works, or by faith? A sharp question. They began in the Spirit; would they end in the flesh?
'O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you?' received ye the Spirit by works, or by faith? A sharp question. They began in the Spirit; would they end in the flesh?
'O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you?' received ye the Spirit by works, or by faith? A sharp question. They began in the Spirit; would they end in the flesh?
Doeth God minister the Spirit and work miracles 'by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?' By faith. God's gifts come through believing.
Doeth God minister the Spirit and work miracles 'by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?' By faith. God's gifts come through believing.
Abraham believed God; 'they which are of faith... are blessed with faithful Abraham.' Faith's children. The believing are Abraham's true seed.
Abraham believed God; 'they which are of faith... are blessed with faithful Abraham.' Faith's children. The believing are Abraham's true seed.
Abraham believed God; 'they which are of faith... are blessed with faithful Abraham.' Faith's children. The believing are Abraham's true seed.
Abraham believed God; 'they which are of faith... are blessed with faithful Abraham.' Faith's children. The believing are Abraham's true seed.
As many as are of works are 'under the curse'; 'the just shall live by faith.' Law condemns. The law curses all who fail to keep it whole.
As many as are of works are 'under the curse'; 'the just shall live by faith.' Law condemns. The law curses all who fail to keep it whole.
As many as are of works are 'under the curse'; 'the just shall live by faith.' Law condemns. The law curses all who fail to keep it whole.
'Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us.' Redeemed. He bore our curse upon the tree.
'Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us.' Redeemed. He bore our curse upon the tree.
The law, coming after, cannot annul the promise to Abraham; the inheritance is 'by promise.' Promise stands. Grace was given before the law.
The law, coming after, cannot annul the promise to Abraham; the inheritance is 'by promise.' Promise stands. Grace was given before the law.
The law, coming after, cannot annul the promise to Abraham; the inheritance is 'by promise.' Promise stands. Grace was given before the law.
The law, coming after, cannot annul the promise to Abraham; the inheritance is 'by promise.' Promise stands. Grace was given before the law.
Wherefore the law? 'added because of transgressions,' till the seed should come. Its purpose. The law was a temporary guardian.
Wherefore the law? 'added because of transgressions,' till the seed should come. Its purpose. The law was a temporary guardian.
Wherefore the law? 'added because of transgressions,' till the seed should come. Its purpose. The law was a temporary guardian.
Wherefore the law? 'added because of transgressions,' till the seed should come. Its purpose. The law was a temporary guardian.
Before faith we were kept under the law, 'our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ.' Pointing to Christ. The law leads us to the Saviour.
Before faith we were kept under the law, 'our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ.' Pointing to Christ. The law leads us to the Saviour.
Before faith we were kept under the law, 'our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ.' Pointing to Christ. The law leads us to the Saviour.
Ye are 'all the children of God by faith'; 'there is neither Jew nor Greek'; ye are Abraham's seed. One in Christ. Faith unites all as God's children.
